meta.name: The Gentleman Scientist
meta.subtitle: Drake's meets Kapital. Soft tailoring, scholarly knitwear, artisan craft.
meta.tagline: A wardrobe for a life of letters and leisure.
meta.hero_quote: Patterns are vocabulary. The more you know, the more interesting your sentences become.
meta.footer_message: Curated with craft for Tim — April 2026

products.name: Navy Herringbone Twill Cotton Games Blazer Mk. I
products.description: THE centerpiece. 3-roll-2, unlined body, soft natural shoulder. Navy herringbone twill cotton from Japan based on 1940s US military cloth. Mother of pearl buttons. Draws from workwear as much as tailoring. Made in Italy.

products.name: Model 269 Harris Tweed Sport Coat
products.description: Japanese tailoring meets Scottish cloth. Handmade in Osaka with Harris Tweed from the Outer Hebrides. Natural shoulder, soft construction. The bridge between Tim's Japan love and scholarly direction.

products.name: Unstructured Blazer in Cotton/Linen
products.description: French atelier, natural materials only. Washed cotton-linen, completely unconstructed. The summer version of the tweed — what you wear reading on a terrace in Provence.
products.maker: De Bonne Facture · Paris, France · Since 2013

products.name: Corduroy Sport Coat
products.description: Modern British-Italian. Wide wale corduroy, unlined, natural shoulder. Tobacco/rust colorway is THE gentleman scientist uniform. Jake Grantham and Alex Petsetakis bridge Savile Row and Naples.

products.name: The Ventile Raincoat
products.description: Manchester-made from Ventile — WWII pilot fabric. Tightly woven cotton swells when wet, becoming waterproof without membranes. Replaces technical shells with heritage that functions in PNW rain.
products.maker: Private White V.C. · Manchester, England · Since 2009

products.name: Slim Fit Oxford Button-Down — White
products.description: Japanese-made Ivy League heritage at an insane price point. Rolled collar, slim fit, single-needle construction. The daily uniform shirt. $79 for quality that competes with $200 shirts.

products.name: Slim Fit Oxford Button-Down — Blue
products.description: The most versatile shirt in the wardrobe. Under a navy sport coat, under a shawl cardigan, alone with moleskin. Japanese construction, American heritage, universal utility.

products.name: Slim Fit Oxford Button-Down — Pink
products.description: Pink oxford says 'I'm confident enough to not care.' Under tweed, under a cricket sweater, with cavalry twill. The quiet signal of a man who dresses with intention.

products.name: Camp Collar Shirt in Organic Linen
products.description: Summer scholarly. Natural/ecru linen, camp collar, relaxed cut. The shirt you wear reading on a terrace in Provence. French organic linen, made in Portugal.
products.maker: De Bonne Facture · Paris, France · Since 2013

products.name: Brushed Flannel Tattersall Shirt
products.description: Autumn/winter scholarly. Brushed cotton flannel with tattersall check. The shirt a Cambridge don wears under his tweed. Soft hand, heritage pattern, made in England.

products.name: Bengal Stripe Broadcloth Shirt
products.description: American-made, old-money quality. Blue/white bengal stripe. The one proper shirt for when occasions demand it. Family-run since 1982, sewn in Far Hills, New Jersey.
products.maker: Mercer & Sons · Far Hills, NJ · Since 1982

products.name: Liberty Print Camp Collar Shirt
products.description: Liberty of London floral on a camp collar — the bridge between Tim's pattern DNA and scholarly direction. Made in Ashland, PA since 1932. Museum-quality textile on a factory-made shirt.

products.name: Birth of the Cool Shetland Crew — Heather
products.description: THE cult knitwear brand. Scottish Shetland wool brushed with NATURAL TEASEL BRUSHES — old-world method creating a soft halo you cannot replicate industrially. Teasel-brushed Shetland IS the scholarly texture.

products.name: Birth of the Cool Shetland Crew — Moss
products.description: Moss green Shetland — the color of the Scottish highlands, the PNW forest floor, Barbour country. Under a tweed sport coat, this IS the gentleman scientist uniform.

products.name: Birth of the Cool Shetland Crew — Rust
products.description: Rust Shetland — autumn's color, warm against navy, striking against cream. The third axis of the Shetland rotation. One for every mood, every season, every sport coat.

products.name: Cable Knit Cricket Sweater
products.description: The most 'learned man of letters' garment that exists. Cable-knit V-neck with college stripe trim. Cream wool. Worn by actual Oxford dons. Welsh-made since 1892.

products.name: Shawl Collar Cardigan in Lambswool
products.description: The at-home layer. Scottish lambswool, made in Hawick since 1874. Shawl collar, deep charcoal. What you wear in the study while reading proofs at dawn. Five generations of Scottish knitwear.

products.name: Fair Isle Vest
products.description: Under a sport coat = instant scholar. Real Fair Isle from the actual Shetland Islands. Patterns that once helped identify returning sailors. Heritage from the source, not the interpretation.

products.name: Cashmere Rollneck Sweater
products.description: The turtleneck intellectual. Navy cashmere, made in Elgin, Scotland since 1797. Under a sport coat for lectures, alone for the evening. Steve Jobs without the austerity.
products.maker: Johnstons of Elgin · Elgin, Scotland · Since 1797

products.name: Aran Cable Knit Sweater
products.description: Handknit on the Aran Islands off Ireland's west coast. Museum-quality craft. Natural cream wool, cable patterns passed through generations. The weekend-at-the-cottage piece. Pairs with moleskin and Paraboot Michaels.
products.maker: Inis Meáin · Aran Islands, Ireland · Since 1976

products.name: OG Painter Pant — Olive Sateen
products.description: The anchor trouser. Olive sateen OG Painter — the silhouette that built Stan Ray's reputation. Hammer loop, tool pockets, triple-stitched seams. Made in USA since 1972. Buy two at this price.

products.name: OG Painter Pant — Olive Herringbone Twill
products.description: Olive herringbone twill — the second pillar. Heritage military green. Under a navy sport coat = the daily uniform perfected. Same proven OG Painter cut.

products.name: Wide Wale Corduroy Trousers — Forest Green
products.description: Already Tim's brand. Japanese-made corduroy in heritage fit. Forest green wide wale — the trouser version of his existing love. Deep pile, satisfying texture.

products.name: Pleated Cavalry Twill Trousers
products.description: What a gentleman wears when moleskin is too casual. Stone/tan cavalry twill, pleated, high-rise. British cloth, proper drape. The trouser that matches everything in the wardrobe.

products.name: Drawstring Trousers in Organic Linen
products.description: Summer. Natural linen, drawstring waist. The 'I'm reading on the terrace' trouser. French craft, Portuguese manufacture, eternal style.
products.maker: De Bonne Facture · Paris, France · Since 2013

products.name: 105 Standard Selvedge Denim
products.description: ONE pair of proper jeans. Japanese selvedge denim, standard fit. For weekends when you're not being scholarly. The 105 is orSlow's signature — the right amount of relaxed.

products.name: Leisure Handsewn Penny Loafer
products.description: The quintessential American scholar shoe. Shell cordovan in Color #8 (burgundy). Handsewn in New England since 1884. Develops a rolling patina that no other leather achieves. The second shoe pillar after Paraboot.

products.name: Comfort Craftsman Boot — Mid Brown
products.description: The autumn/winter shoe. One piece of pull-up leather, no seams. Comfort innersole, non-slip rubber outsole. Pairs with moleskin + tweed perfectly. Australian heritage since 1932.

products.name: Blucher Moc
products.description: Japanese craftsmen channeling Americana. Handsewn by Yuki Matsuda's team. Bridges Tim's Japan DNA with New England tradition. Natural chromexcel leather, camp sole.

products.name: Velvet Evening Slipper
products.description: THE leisure shoe. THE single most 'gentleman of leisure' item in existence. Velvet upper, leather sole. Custom embroidery available — get a mathematical symbol or Kapital smiley. Wear at home. Wear to dinner.
products.maker: Stubbs & Wootton · Palm Beach, FL · Since 1993

products.name: Blucher Moc
products.description: Handmade in Lewiston, Maine. True moccasin construction — one piece of leather wrapped around your foot. Camp sole for versatility. The summer casual with provenance.

products.name: Westminster 2 Wool Dressing Gown — Navy
products.description: The morning ritual garment. Fine wool, shawl collar, piped edges. This is what you wear while reading proofs at 7am. London-made luxury for the at-home gentleman.

products.name: Cashmere Bed Socks
products.description: The luxury nobody sees. Cashmere bed socks for the study, for late-night theorem work. Scottish-made since 1797. Pure indulgence for the feet.
products.maker: Johnstons of Elgin · Elgin, Scotland · Since 1797

products.name: Linen Pyjamas Set
products.description: Proper sleepwear. European linen, relaxed fit. Not a t-shirt and boxers. Copenhagen design, Portuguese manufacture. A gentleman's nightwear.

products.name: Herbrand Optical Frames
products.description: Handmade in London. Each frame named after a London street. Scholarly without being stuffy. The single most transformative accessory — instant visual shift to 'man of letters.'

products.name: Hand-Rolled Linen Pocket Square
products.description: White linen, hand-rolled edges. The finishing touch under a sport coat. Not matchy — deliberately askew. The TV fold or the casual puff. Made in England.

products.name: Printed Silk Pocket Square
products.description: Screen-printed silk in paisley or geometric. Color that peeks from the breast pocket. The gentleman's subtle signal. Hand-rolled in England.

products.name: Wool Challis Pocket Square
products.description: Wool challis — the autumn/winter pocket square. Matte finish, rich colors, no sheen. Pairs with tweed without competing. The scholar's choice.

products.name: Arran Cashmere Scarf
products.description: Scottish-made cashmere. The autumn/winter accessory. Paisley or geometric patterns. Signals 'I read books.' Woven on looms in Ayrshire, Scotland since 1866.

products.name: Tangente 38
products.description: Bauhaus design, in-house Alpha movement. Made in Glashütte, Germany's watchmaking capital. The Tangente is THE scientist's watch — precise, minimal, intellectual. Not flashy. Just correct.

products.name: Card Case in Natural Cowhide
products.description: Vegetable-tanned Tuscan leather that ages into rich honey. Founded by Wanny Di Filippo in Florence, 1970. Natural cowhide — no dyes, no shortcuts. Patina is autobiography.

products.name: Lamy 2000 Fountain Pen
products.description: Bauhaus design from 1966 — unchanged because it was already perfect. Makrolon body, 14K gold nib, piston fill. The gentleman scientist writes by hand. German precision meets industrial design.

products.name: Leather Working Tote
products.description: American-made in Providence, RI. Unlined, unstructured, beautiful patina. Carries books, notebooks, a laptop. Not a tech backpack. Vegetable-tanned leather that tells your story.

products.name: Classic Bifold Wallet — Natural
products.description: Portland, Oregon. Vegetable-tanned English bridle leather. PNW connection — made blocks from Powell's Books. Natural leather ages from blonde to rich amber over years.
